metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: The Black Friday period is used by many Dutch people to make purchases, often with a view to Sinterklaas and Christmas. During this period, consumers spend considerably more than in the past, when the days around Sinterklaas were still real purchasing days. To avoid peak crowds, more retailers are starting to make offers earlier in November, with customers mainly lured via the internet.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: Read all the facts and developments in the analysis 'Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases'.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: 131 KB](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/black-friday-snoept-mee-van-sinterklaas-aankopen.pdf)

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: Black Friday, an annual shopping event that originated in the United States, is held on the day after Thanksgiving, the fourth Thursday in November. It marks the casual start of the holiday shopping season and is known for great deals. In recent years, the event has become increasingly popular worldwide. Since around 2015, Dutch retailers have also been participating in Black Friday, making the phenomenon increasingly visible to the Dutch public. How much extra consumer expenditure is actually made around this 'bargain day'?

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: For this research we use a consumer survey and anonymized and aggregated transaction data from ABN AMRO. We analyze how much additional consumer expenditure households make during the period surrounding Black Friday in the years 2016 to 2024. For 2025 we have included the most recent data up to and including Black Friday.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: The holidays remain popular among Dutch people. Consumer research by ABN AMRO and research agency Q&A Retail with 2,000 respondents shows that 80 percent of Dutch people celebrate Christmas and 48 percent Sinterklaas. Of those who celebrate Christmas, 72 percent plan to buy gifts. For Sinterklaas that percentage is even 85 percent.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: Black Friday and Cyber ​​Monday are playing an increasingly important role in preparations for the holidays. Only 23 percent of consumers do not expect to do any shopping for Sinterklaas or Christmas during these promotional days. In 2023 that was still 27 percent, according to the consumer survey.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: Young adults up to the age of 35 in particular use these discount moments to do their festive shopping. Young adults celebrate the holidays more often than older generations and they also buy gifts for these holidays more often. This is part of the explanation why younger generations make more purchases than older people on Black Friday.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: Nearly 37 percent of consumers use Black Friday and Cyber ​​Monday to buy gifts for both Sinterklaas and Christmas. It is striking that the share that purchases exclusively for Sinterklaas remains relatively low, despite the fact that the promotional days take place just before December 5. Only 12 percent focus exclusively on Sinterklaas during these promotional days, while 29 percent use the promotions exclusively for Christmas.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: ## Households spend significantly more on Black Friday

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: In the anonymized ABN AMRO transaction data we indeed see a clear peak in consumption on Black Friday. The graph below shows that between 2016 and 2024, households spent an average of 25 percent more on Black Friday compared to the average consumption in the weeks before the Black Friday period. We call this the 'Black Friday consumption effect'. We see higher consumer expenditure not only on Black Friday itself, but also on the days surrounding it. For example, we see a gradual build-up in the days before Black Friday, and a small additional peak on 'Cyber ​​Monday', the Monday after the weekend, where traditionally a lot of online sales of electronics take place. The days after Cyber ​​Monday still show increased consumption, which can largely be explained by the approaching holidays, with Sinterklaas being the first in the list.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: This year the peak on Black Friday is somewhat stronger than average, with approximately 30 percent extra consumption. What is more striking is that the Wednesday and Thursday preceding Black Friday were already almost at the same level as Black Friday itself. The year 2025 therefore appears to have been a strong year for Black Friday purchases. Because Black Friday fell quite late this year, this was actually predictable.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: When Black Friday falls late in November, this has two consequences. Firstly, the average Dutch person has already received his salary for a few days. This year the payout fell on Tuesday. Payment of salary always leads to extra consumption, something we correct for in our analysis. This year, the 'extra consumption' due to Black Friday was approximately 5 percent on the salary day itself, while the following Wednesday and Thursday showed a lot of extra consumption (see the first graph below). So purchases really started after receiving a salary. A second consequence of Black Friday falling late in the month is that the shopping day falls shortly before Sinterklaas, meaning there is no extra weekend to make Sinterklaas purchases.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: ## The timing of Black Friday in relation to Sinterklaas matters

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: The number of days between Black Friday and Sinterklaas varies every year. Black Friday always falls on the day after Thanksgiving, while Sinterklaas is mainly celebrated on December 5. When Black Friday falls on November 23, the earliest date in November it can fall, there are 12 days between the two days. In contrast, only 6 days remain when Black Friday falls on November 29, the last date Black Friday can fall in November.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: This variation has important consequences for consumption behavior. When Black Friday is closer to Sinterklaas, purchases are more concentrated around Black Friday; consumption is on average almost 50 percent higher on Black Friday itself, compared to approximately 15 percent when there is an extra weekend between Black Friday and Sinterklaas. In other words, the 'Black Friday consumption effect' is significantly strengthened without an extra weekend.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: ## Black Friday and Cyber Monday are primarily an online phenomenon

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: While Black Friday in the US was originally a primarily offline phenomenon, our analysis shows that sales now take place largely online. In the next graph below we analyze the total additional consumption in the period around Black Friday, again averaged over 2016-2024, where we make a breakdown between online and offline purchases. Just like the graphs above, the data is expressed in average consumption in one day; 200 percent therefore implies that the total additional consumption amounts to the amount normally consumed in two days. It turns out that the 'Black Friday consumption effect' applies to both online and offline purchases, with visiting stores being more popular when Black Friday falls close to Sinterklaas. In that case, expenditure in physical stores is almost three times higher than with an extra weekend, while online purchases are only twice as high.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: The right-hand graph above shows that the share of extra consumption on Black Friday itself and in the week before Black Friday has increased in recent years, at the expense of the period between Black Friday and Sinterklaas. While the share of extra consumption between Cyber ​​Monday and Sinterklaas was still 60 percent in 2016, this will have fallen to less than 30 percent in 2024. Adjusted for inflation, we see no serious increase or decrease in total additional consumption over this period; we only see a shift in the moment of purchase. From 2019 onwards, Black Friday (the day itself) took off, especially during the corona period. Since 2022, we have seen that the peak on Black Friday itself has been greatly reduced, with a further spread of purchases to the week before Black Friday.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: Nowadays, some stores start Black Friday promotions around Singles Day on November 11, or even start Black November promotions from November 1. An important reason for the early start is to spread the crowds, so that parcel deliverers can better handle the large peak in the last quarter of the year. This longer period also makes it easier for consumers to spread out their holiday shopping.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: The results for 2025 confirm all these previous patterns. We see that Black Friday has gone from a steep peak to a flat plateau of an extended shopping period. Purchases started after salary receipt, and seem to have been spread out quite well. The total extra consumption seems high compared to the historical average, but is largely consistent with other years in which there was no extra weekend for Sinterklaas. Many of these purchases are found in the shoe today.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: In this analysis we share relevant economic insights from ABN AMRO's aggregated and anonymized transaction data. We use transaction data to gain better insight into economic flows. We only use aggregated data and anonymized data for static research. The results of the research cannot be traced back to a natural person and are exclusively intended for research into economic trends.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: In addition, this publication uses results from a consumer survey. The analyzes based on transaction data and consumer research were carried out independently of each other; no data is linked.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: The consumer survey was conducted in collaboration with Q&A Retail among 2,000 respondents, divided into five age categories: 18 to 25 years, 26 to 39 years, 40 to 54 years, 55 to 69 years and 70 to 81 years. Income differences have also been included, ranging from respondents with (much) less to (much) more income than the Dutch average.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: The analysis of spending is based on daily transaction data for the period from 2016 to Black Friday 2025. For this study, we focused on consumer spending, which we selected based on the payment method used. This includes transactions via PIN, iDEAL, cash withdrawals and e-commerce payments. Transactions known not to involve consumer expenditure are excluded. This includes transactions related to loans, investments, internal transfers, payments to the tax authorities and fixed costs.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: To make an accurate estimate of the percentage of 'extra consumption', we corrected the daily data for consumption periodicity effects within the week; for example, more is always consumed on a Friday than on a Monday. In addition, the timing of Black Friday in relation to the payment of salaries plays an important role in the level of consumption. To do this, we correct consumption on the day of and the weekend after receipt of salary based on historical patterns - patterns that fall outside Black Friday. Finally, 'extra consumption' was calculated as a percentage of extra consumption compared to the average daily consumption in the three weeks before the Black Friday period. This is recalculated every year and therefore corrects for overall consumption levels and price levels.

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

metadata.source_title: Black Friday benefits from Sinterklaas purchases

text: Today's store is more than just a pantry. It offers customers advice and inspiration and is the place to be in contact with customers. Due to the Ukraine crisis and significant inflation, consumer confidence has fallen sharply and this affects consumer behavior. Retailers are also faced with significant cost increases in the areas of purchasing, rent, personnel costs, Covid recovery payments and energy. Circumstances remain challenging for many retailers even after Covid.
